citations.narrative:
In the kitchen, there is spoiled produce being stored in the refrigerator and on the counters. Inside the refrigerator, there
are strawberries covered in a fuzzy growth and bananas that have darkly discolored and shriveled peels. The bottom of
the refrigerator has a puddle of dark fluid. On the counter, there is a box of unrefrigerated produce that has attracted
numerous fruit flies. There is no separation between what is to be fed to the gibbons and what is for disposal.
Spoiled perishable items can further compromise other supplies of food stored in close proximity to them or attract
unwanted pests as they continue to degrade. Ingestion of contaminated feed can cause illness in the animals. Supplies of
food and bedding must be stored in a manner that protects the supplies from spoilage, contamination, and vermin
infestation. The supplies must be stored off the floor and away from the walls, to allow cleaning underneath and around
the supplies. Food requiring refrigeration must be stored accordingly, and all food must be stored a manner that prevents
contamination and deterioration of its nutritive value.
To be corrected by May 1, 2026.

citations.desc: Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control.
citations.narrative:
There is not an adequate pest control program for the kitchen building. During the inspection, a live rat was seen going
through a hole that had been chewed in the wall. The floor of the kitchen has an accumulation of rodent feces and there is
evidence of rodents chewing through insulation.
Pests such as rats can spread disease and contaminate food, surfaces, and animal housing areas. An effective program
for control of insects, external parasites affecting nonhuman primates, and birds and mammals that are pests, must be
established and maintained so as to promote the health and well-being of the animals and reduce contamination by pests
in animal areas.
To be corrected by May 8, 2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the licensee.
n

narrative:
3.75(e) 
   
Housing facilities, general. 
In the kitchen, there is spoiled produce being stored in the refrigerator and on the counters. Inside the refrigerator, there 
are strawberries covered in a fuzzy growth and bananas that have darkly discolored and shriveled peels. The bottom of 
the refrigerator has a puddle of dark fluid. On the counter, there is a box of unrefrigerated produce that has attracted 
numerous fruit flies. There is no separation between what is to be fed to the gibbons and what is for disposal. 
 
Spoiled perishable items can further compromise other supplies of food stored in close proximity to them or attract 
unwanted pests as they continue to degrade. Ingestion of contaminated feed can cause illness in the animals. Supplies of 
food and bedding must be stored in a manner that protects the supplies from spoilage, contamination, and vermin 
infestation. The supplies must be stored off the floor and away from the walls, to allow cleaning underneath and around 
the supplies. Food requiring refrigeration must be stored accordingly, and all food must be stored a manner that prevents 
contamination and deterioration of its nutritive value.  
To be corrected by May 1, 2026. 
3.84(d) 
   
Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control. 
There is not an adequate pest control program for the kitchen building. During the inspection, a live rat was seen going 
through a hole that had been chewed in the wall. The floor of the kitchen has an accumulation of rodent feces and there is 
evidence of rodents chewing through insulation. 
 
Pests such as rats can spread disease and contaminate food, surfaces, and animal housing areas. An effective program 
for control of insects, external parasites affecting nonhuman primates, and birds and mammals that are pests, must be 
established and maintained so as to promote the health and well-being of the animals and reduce contamination by pests 
in animal areas. 
To be corrected by May 8, 2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the licensee.
